description: An animated sequence shows blue 3D text spelling out "Amazing Discoveries" against changing backgrounds. Upbeat instrumental theme music plays.

On a television screen, a man in a colorful patterned sweater stands on a set next to Joey's character, who wears a plaid shirt. The man in the sweater opens his arms and says, "Welcome, everybody. Welcome to Amazing Discoveries."
The setting changes to a living room. Ross, Chandler, Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe are sitting on or near a sofa, looking toward the television.
Phoebe points forward and says, "Oh, we're again," as Joey, wearing an arm sling and standing behind the sofa, gestures with his uninjured hand and says, "you guys can please not watch this."
Joey lowers his hand.
On the television, the man in the sweater addresses the camera, "Folks, this ever happened to you?"
The TV shows Joey's character struggling to open a milk carton, pulling at its top. The man in the sweater gestures and says, "You go to the refrigerator to get a nice flash to melt."
Joey's character continues struggling with the carton. The man in the sweater says, "But these darn carts are so sling and slang and hard to open. Well, you"
Joey's character rips the carton open, spilling milk onto the counter, and says, "said it, Mike. I don't"
Joey's character on the TV stands holding the torn carton, looking down at the spilled milk.
On the TV, Joey's character gestures to the spill and says, "there's got to be a better way." Mike points toward him and says, "And there is. Kevin,"
Joey, standing in the living room, gestures forward and says, "take these tennis off." Ross, seated on the sofa, replies, "No way."
Ross turns his head slightly and says, "Kevin."
Phoebe laughs while the group continues watching the TV.
On the TV, Mike sweeps his arms wide and says, "there's a revolutionary new product that guarantees you'll never have to open up milk cartons again."
Mike picks up a packaged tool with a spout and holds it up beside his head, adding, "Meet the milkmaster."
Joey's character on the TV smiles widely and says, "2000," while Mike holds the product beside him.
Joey's character maintains his wide smile.
In the living room, Ross looks back toward Joey and asks, "Are you intrigued?" Chandler turns his head and says, "You're flinging, flinging. Right, I am." Joey lowers his head.
Joey turns and walks away from the sofa.
On the TV, Mike hands a packaged tool to Joey's character and says, "First time. You've never used this product before? You're going to see how easy this is to do. Go ahead."
Joey's character presses the tool into the top of a new milk carton. Mike points at the carton and says, "This works in any milk carton."
Joey's character smiles, looking at the carton, and says, "Wow, it is easy."
Joey's character pours milk from the carton's spout into a glass, saying, "Now I can have milk every day."
Both men on the TV smile widely. In the living room, the group on the sofa laughs while Joey, standing behind them, shakes his head.
Joey gestures with his hand and says, "I can't watch this anymore." Phoebe, holding a remote control, points it toward the TV and says, "All right, you've suffered enough. We'll change it."

Phoebe repeatedly presses buttons on the remote, saying, "We'll change it." The TV screen switches to a black-and-white scene of a man in a suit at a desk, as an off-screen voice says, "How many times has this happened to you?"
The man on the TV struggles to pull apart a tangled tape measure.
The man on the TV throws the tape measure down on the desk. In the living room, Joey raises his uninjured hand and says, "There's got to be a better way."
Joey lowers his hand and looks down while the group on the sofa laughs.
The setting changes to a kitchen, where Joey sits alone at a small table, resting his chin on his hand. The screen then goes black.
The screen remains black in silence.
An exterior daytime view shows a dense city skyline with skyscrapers under a clear blue sky.
In a coffee shop, Monica, Chandler, Phoebe, and Janice sit around a low table. Phoebe writes on a notepad. Monica says, "It's official."
Monica holds a yellow mug and continues, "There are no good movies." Chandler holds an open newspaper.
Janice turns to Chandler and says, "So let's go to a bad one and make out." Chandler looks at her and smiles.
Janice leans in and kisses Chandler on the lips. Monica, sitting next to them, looks away and holds her mug against her torso.
Monica shifts her body away, raising one hand, and says, "Perhaps you would like me to turn like this so you can bunny bump against my back." Janice smiles.
Joey, wearing his arm sling, walks into the coffee shop and approaches the group's table. Production credits appear at the bottom of the screen.
Joey stops near the table. Chandler says, "Hey, Med. What's up?" Joey places his hand on his hip and replies, "Maybe you can tell me."
Joey gestures with his hand as he says, "My agent would like to know why I didn't show up at the audition I didn't know I had. Today the first"
Joey points toward Chandler and continues, "good thing she gets me in weeks. How could you not give me the message?"
Chandler looks up at Joey, keeping the newspaper in his lap.
Chandler lowers his newspaper slightly and says, "I'll tell you, I do enjoy guilt."
Joey looks at Chandler as Janice turns her head toward Chandler.
Chandler continues, "But it wasn't me." Phoebe points her pen toward Chandler and says, "Yes, it was. It was him."
Phoebe nods, holding her pen extended toward Chandler. Chandler briefly looks at Phoebe before turning his gaze downward.
Phoebe rapidly raises both hands to her head and says, "Okay, it was me."
Joey stares steadily at Phoebe. Phoebe lowers her hands to the table.
Joey asks, "How was it you?" Phoebe touches her hands to her head again and begins, "Well, it was just it was also crazy, you know?"
Phoebe gestures with one hand as she continues, "I mean, Chandler was in the closet counting to ten and he was up to"
Phoebe spreads both hands outward, adding, "seven and I hadn't found a place to hide yet."
Joey stands with his hand on his hip, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe stands up, steps toward Joey, and holds her open palm in front of his face, saying, "I meant to tell you, and I wrote it all down on my hand. See? All of it."
Joey looks closely at her palm and replies, "Yes, that's my audition." Phoebe gestures toward her palm with her other fingers.
Monica leans her head back and smiles.
Monica raises a hand and says, "See? Now this is why I keep notepads everywhere." Chandler points toward Monica and replies, "Yes, and that's why we don't invite you to play."
Phoebe steps backward and lowers her arms, while Joey stands looking downward.
Janice looks at Joey and asks, "What is the great tragedy here?"
Janice gestures with her hand, saying, "You go get yourself another appointment." Joey shakes his head and replies, "Well, Estelle tried. You know."
Joey continues, "The casting director told her I missed my chance." Phoebe turns to face Joey and says, "Well, that is"
Phoebe steps toward Joey again and says, "unfair. I'll call her. I'll tell her it was totally my fault."
Joey shakes his head and says, "She can't do that. The casting director doesn't talk to friends."
Joey continues, "She only talks to agents."
Phoebe, holding a small black device, looks downward and says, "What a sad little life. She must"
Joey looks down with his brow furrowed, his mouth closed.
In a coffee shop, Phoebe holds a cell phone to her ear. Joey, wearing a sling on his left arm, sits nearby and asks, "OOH. What are you doing? What are you know?" Phoebe replies, "I know."
Phoebe looks forward, maintaining her hold on the phone.
Phoebe speaks into the phone, "Hi. This is Caitlin from Phoebe Buffet's office."
Joey turns his head toward Phoebe.
Joey gestures with his right hand as he looks at Phoebe. Phoebe continues on the phone, "Is Anne there for Phoebe? She'll know what it's about."
Joey steps closer to Phoebe.
Joey stands and reaches toward Phoebe. He says, "Hang on. Hang up."
Joey attempts to grab the phone. Phoebe pulls away, keeping it out of his reach.
Phoebe turns her back to Joey and says into the phone, "Annie hot. Listen, we've got a problem with Joey Tribiani. Apparently, he missed his audition. Who did you speak to in"
Phoebe continues, "my office? Estelle." Joey stands behind her, pointing a finger.
Phoebe gestures with her free hand, saying, "No, I don't know what I'm going to do with her. No. All right. So your husband leaves you and then burns down the"
Phoebe finishes, "apartment. The world does not stop."
Chandler sits on the couch next to Monica and Janice.
Chandler looks around and asks, "Is anybody else scared?"
Joey stands with his hands near his waist, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe gestures with her left hand and says into the phone, "Right. Well, look, if Joey loses this audition, then that is it for us."
Phoebe adds, "Still, I don't care."
Phoebe lowers her hand and listens.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Annie, you are a doll. What time can you see him? I need a"
Phoebe pats her pockets, looking for something. Chandler begins patting his own pockets while seated on the couch.
Phoebe leans over Chandler, patting his head and shoulders. Chandler grimaces and lowers his head.
Chandler gestures broadly and says, "woman. A pad. Get the woman a pad. A pad. A pad."
Phoebe holds a pen and writes directly on her left hand.
Chandler raises his hands and says, "Oh, now you want a pad," his volume rising.
Chandler rubs the top of his head. The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building at a street corner.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen and living area. Ross sits at a table drinking from a mug while reading a newspaper. Rachel stands holding a laundry basket.
Rachel walks toward the window with the laundry basket, looks out, and says, "Well, that's funny. Ugly Naked Guy's still naked, but his dog's wearing a sweater."
Ross looks up. The apartment door opens, and Carol and Susan enter, followed by a young boy.
Ross stands, approaches the boy, and says, "Here's my boy."
Ross picks the boy up and says, "Here's my boy. And here's his barbie." He holds a Barbie doll in his left hand.
Ross holds the boy and gestures with his left hand, holding the doll. Carol and Susan stand nearby, smiling.
Ross gestures with his right hand and asks, "What's? What's my boy doing with the Barbie?"
Susan turns her head toward Carol.
Susan says, "He picked it out at the toy store himself."
Carol steps forward, gesturing with her hands, and says, "He loves it. He carries it everywhere. It's like a security blanket but with ski boots and a kicky beret."
Rachel places a folded towel on the table.
Ross looks down slightly and says, "Yeah, it's cute."
Ross looks at Carol and asks, "Why does he have it again? So it's got a doll. So what?"
Ross gestures with his right hand while holding the boy.
Susan says, "Unless you're afraid he's going to grow up to be in show business."
Ross looks toward Susan.
Carol says, "Wouldn't have anything to do with that being raised by two women, would it?"
Ross looks down, his expression unchanging.
Ross looks back up and says, "You know what? It's fine."
Ross says, "If you're okay with the Barbie thing, so am I. Give Daddy the barbie."
Ross kneels, placing the boy in a small chair.
Ross holds up a monster truck toy and asks, "Okay, how about don't you want to play with a monster truck?"
Ross moves the toy truck around, making engine noises. The boy watches while holding the Barbie doll.
Ross continues, "Okay."
Ross picks up small dinosaur figures and asks, "How about a Dino soldier?"
Ross makes roaring noises and moves the dinosaur figures. The boy keeps his hands on the Barbie doll.
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "Ross, you are so pathetic. Why can't your son just play with his doll?"
Ross looks at Rachel. Joey enters the apartment holding a piece of paper, and Ross looks back toward the boy.
Monica walks into the living room from a doorway, wearing a pink cardigan and a patterned skirt.
Monica says, "I gotta go to work."
Monica holds her hands out, palms up, looking around the room.
Still holding her hands out, Monica asks, "Has anybody seen my left boob?"
Rachel, standing in the kitchen in a blue sweater, turns to look at Monica. Joey lies back on an armchair with his right arm in a sling.
Joey smiles, keeping his head resting on the chair, and says, "I love that movie."
Monica walks over to Joey. She reaches behind his back on the armchair and pulls out a padded bra insert.
Holding the insert, Monica says, "It is joey, what are you—" as Joey overlaps with, "I'm sorry,"
Joey continues, "Just felt nice," shifting his gaze.
Monica stuffs the insert into her cardigan. A phone ringing is audible as the apartment door opens.
Chandler enters quickly, carrying a cordless phone, and says, "Joey. Joe, answer the phone."
Joey gestures with his left hand, saying, "Hey, I only got one good arm. You know, you should be doing stuff for me. Go get me a sweater."
Chandler lowers the phone, his jaw tight.
Chandler steps forward and says, "Just do it, okay?" his volume rising slightly.
Chandler paces slightly, explaining, "It's Janice. And if I get it, I'm going to have to see her tonight."
Chandler looks down at the ringing phone in his hand.
The ringing stops. Chandler says, "Oh, that's great."
Chandler raises his hands briefly and adds, "I'm going to have to see her tonight."
Rachel, sitting at the kitchen table, asks, "What's the idea? Why don't you want to see Janice?"
Chandler replies, "Okay. Last night at dinner, when the meals came she put half her"
Chandler gestures with his free hand, continuing, "chicken picotta on my plate. And then she took all my tomatoes."
Monica and Ross, standing side by side, stare at Chandler.
Ross asks, "That's bad because," and Monica adds, "you hate chicken."
Chandler looks at them.
Chandler says, "No."
Ross suggests, "You didn't want to share your tomatoes. Tomatoes are very important to you."
Chandler gestures broadly, his pace quickening as he says, "No, it's like all of a sudden we were this couple, okay?"
Chandler continues, "And this alarm started going off. My know, run for"
Chandler waves his arm, finishing, "your life. Get out of the building."
Monica looks at Ross.
Rachel, sitting with her arms crossed, says, "Men are unbelievable."
Rachel continues, "Janice just thought she was giving you chicken."
Rachel concludes, "She didn't think she was giving you scary chicken."
Chandler looks at Rachel, then turns toward Monica.
Monica gestures toward Chandler, asking, "What is it with you people? I mean, the minute you start to feel something, you have to run away."
Chandler replies, "I know. That"
Chandler steps closer to Monica.
Chandler continues, his hands gesturing, "that's why I don't want to go tonight. I'm afraid I'm going to say something"
Chandler keeps his hands raised slightly.
Chandler finishes, "stupid."
Monica points at him and asks, "You mean like that guy thing where you act all mean and distant until you get us to break up with you?"
Joey, sitting up slightly, points and says, "Hey, you know about that."
Chandler looks back at Monica.
Chandler holds his hands out, saying, "What do I do? I want to get past this. I don't want to be afraid of the commitment thing."
Chandler gestures forward with his hand, adding, "I want to go through the tunnel to the other side"
Ross sits on the arm of the chair next to Joey.
Ross finishes for him, "where there's no fear of commitment."
Chandler looks at Ross.
Chandler asks, "Do we have any"
Chandler pauses, resting his hand over his mouth briefly before lowering it.
Chandler gestures widely, finishing, "do we have any thoughts here?"
Joey gestures with his left hand, saying, "Well, I've never been through the tunnel myself because as I understand it. You're not allowed to go through with more than one girl in the"
Joey finishes, "car. Right?"
Chandler stands with his hands on his hips, his gaze fixed forward.
Joey leans forward, gesturing with his hand. "But it seems to me it's pretty much like anything else. Face your fear."
Joey continues, "You have a fear of heights. You go to the"
Joey points upward and finishes, "top of the building. You're afraid of bugs."
Joey lowers his hand, keeping his gaze on Chandler.
Joey says, "Get a bug."
Chandler looks on, his mouth pressed into a line.
Joey points at Chandler, emphasizing his words. "In this case, you have a fear of commitment. So I say you go in there and you be the most committed guy there ever was."
Monica points at Joey, saying, "Amazingly, that makes sense."
Chandler covers his mouth with his hand, asking, "You think?" Joey nods, "Oh, yeah."
Joey gestures with an open hand, urging, "Go for it, man."
Joey points an index finger upward, adding, "Jump off the high dive."
Joey points a finger gun, saying, "Stare down the barrel of the gun."
Joey makes a throwing gesture and says, "Pee into the wind."
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "Yeah, joe, I assure you, if I'm staring down the barrel of a gun I'm going to be pretty much peeing every which way."
Joey lowers his hand. The setting changes to the exterior window of a coffee shop; a painted sign reads "CENTRAL PERK" with two coffee cups. People are visible inside.
Inside the coffee shop, Phoebe sits on a sofa holding an acoustic guitar. Joey and Chandler are seated nearby. Phoebe says, "You guys, listen to this and tell me what you think. Okay?"
Phoebe strums a single chord on the guitar.
Chandler, looking downward, says, "That's interesting."

Inside a coffee shop, Phoebe sits on a sofa holding an acoustic guitar across her lap, examining a small black object in her hand. Joey stands nearby wearing an arm sling. Background chatter is audible.
The device beeps. Phoebe looks at it and says, "Oh, because it's a beeper."
Phoebe continues holding the beeper, keeping her gaze directed at it.
Phoebe points the beeper toward Joey and says, "Oh, it's your audition from this morning."
Phoebe begins to stand up, holding the guitar.

Phoebe walks toward Rachel, who is seated nearby. "Can I use the phone again?" Phoebe asks. Rachel picks up a cloth and replies, "Sure, babes. You know, that's what it's there for. Emergencies and pretend agents."
Rachel stands and walks away. Joey approaches Phoebe, clasping his hands together.
Joey bounces slightly and says, "Come on, baby. Come on," while Phoebe dials a large black cordless phone.
Phoebe lifts the phone to her ear.
Holding the phone, Phoebe says, "Hi, I have Phoebe Buffet returning a page. Okay, well, she's in her car. I'll have to pat you through." Joey stands closely beside her.
Joey smiles broadly at Phoebe.
Joey says softly, "Very nice touch."
Phoebe lowers the phone, shakes her free hand, and adjusts her posture.
Phoebe places the phone back to her ear and says, "Okay, go ahead."
Phoebe clears her throat and changes her stance, preparing to speak.
In a slightly higher pitch, Phoebe says, "Hi, Annie."
Phoebe listens, maintaining her grip on the phone.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Fantastic. You got it."
Rachel approaches from behind and throws her arms around Joey, both grinning and swaying slightly.
Phoebe continues speaking into the phone, "Oh, okay. Will he work for scale, you ask me? Well, I don't know about that, except that I do and he will."
Phoebe nods slightly while listening.
Phoebe says, "Great."
Phoebe smiles widely and says, "Oh, you are such a sweetheart. I would love to have lunch with you."
Phoebe says, "How about we have lunch next? Went through a tunnel." She makes a static noise with her mouth, rapidly folds down the phone antenna, and lowers the phone.
Joey hugs Rachel.
Joey turns to Phoebe, clasping his hands. "Unbelievable. Thank you so much," he says.
Phoebe smiles and replies, "It was really fun."
Phoebe brushes her hair back with one hand.
Phoebe says, "I mean, I've never talked on a car phone before,"
Joey gestures with his free hand and steps closer to Phoebe.
Speaking quickly, Joey says, "so amazing. Could you do me this huge favor? See, there's this one other audition that I really, really want and Estelle couldn't get me"
Phoebe shakes her head and replies, "in. I don't know. I mean, it was fun one time, but come on."
Joey interrupts, gesturing with his hand. "Please? It would just be this one more. Well, actually it's two," he says.
Phoebe asks, "Two?" Joey nods and says, "Yeah."
Joey adjusts his posture and says, "Well, really it's three. You're so good at it. I love you."
Phoebe stares at Joey, her facial muscles relaxing.
Phoebe says, "Okay, I'll do it. But just these three, right?" Joey quickly responds, "No, it's four."
The setting changes to the exterior of a multi-story apartment building at night.
Inside an apartment, Chandler and Janice sit at a foosball table converted into a dining table. Janice asks, "So how come you wanted to eat in tonight?"
Chandler reaches beside his chair.
Chandler pulls a wrapped rectangular box onto the table and hands it to her, saying, "Because I wanted to give you this."
Janice takes the box. "Oh, are you a puppy?" she asks.
Janice unties the ribbon and lifts the lid off the box.
Janice pulls out a roll of patterned paper and says, "Contact paper."
Chandler smiles faintly.
Chandler says, "You never really know what to say when someone you're sleeping with gives you contact paper."
Chandler leans forward slightly.
Chandler points at the paper and says, "Wait, there's more. See, the contact paper is to go in"
Chandler reaches under the table.
Chandler lifts a wooden drawer from below the table, sets it on the glass surface, and says, "your brand new drawer."
Janice looks at the drawer with her mouth slightly open.
Chandler points downward and says, "See, the drawer actually goes in my dresser."
Janice smiles with parted lips. "Oh, you didn't have to do this," she says.
Chandler stands up, stepping back from the table. "Yes, I did," he replies.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Yes, I did. Because you're my girlfriend."
Chandler puts his hands on his hips and adds, "And that's what girlfriends should get."
Janice looks up at him, her smile widening.
Janice stands up and says, "Well, I gotta buy a vowel"
Janice steps closer to Chandler.
Janice adds, "because oh, my God."
Janice wraps her arms around Chandler's neck and kisses him on the lips. Chandler returns the kiss.
Janice holds Chandler's face with one hand and says, "Who would have thought that someday Chandler Bing would buy me a drawer?"
Chandler's mouth forms a stiff line.
Chandler replies, "Not me."
Chandler averts his gaze slightly, his jaw visibly tight.
Chandler looks back at her and says, "But that's what's happened. And there's more."
Chandler pauses and shifts his weight.
Chandler points a finger forward and says, "We should take a trip."
Janice steps back slightly, her eyes widening. "It we should?" she asks.
Chandler nods rapidly and gestures with stiff, rapid movements. "Yes. We're a couple, and that's what couples do. And I want to meet your parents," he says quickly.
Chandler raises his finger again and says rapidly, "We should take a trip with your parents."
Janice stares at Chandler with her mouth wide open. Chandler stares back, his jaw remaining tight.
Inside an apartment, Janice stands holding a small plastic cup and a straw. She looks toward Chandler, who is off-screen.
Janice says, "I don't think we need to because you're tripping me out right now." Her voice is nasal and she keeps her gaze fixed on Chandler while speaking, holding the cup near her chest.
Janice remains still, looking toward Chandler. Chandler is now visible, facing her with his lips pressed together and blinking several times. A soft instrumental melody is audible in the background.

The setting changes to a coffee shop. Phoebe stands holding a black cordless phone to her right ear with her mouth slightly open. Ross, Monica, Joey, and Rachel are seated in the background. The sound of audience laughter is audible.
